executiveSummary.text: S&P 500 logged best monthly gain in years (+10% in April) on AI earnings momentum, closing at record 7,230. April 29 FOMC held rates at 3.5-3.75% — unchanged for third consecutive meeting.
Oil collapsed ~10% in days after Iran declared Strait of Hormuz open (April 17) under ceasefire, easing inflation fears that had gripped markets in March. WTI ~$100.69 as of May 3 vs $111+ peak.

Trump escalated EU trade tension — raised auto tariffs to 25% on May 1, tearing up partial deal. This is a 2nd-order risk: EU slowdown → weaker euro → stronger USD → SGD heads toward 1.28+ (tail risk for SGD-denominated portfolio).

TSMC Q1 profit +58% YoY; guiding $52-56B capex for 2026. AI infrastructure buildout remains the dominant earnings theme. NVDA fiscal Q4 $68.1B revenue (Guiding Q1 FY26: $78B — reports May 20). AI Infra thesis intact.
Uranium/small-modular-reactor stocks surged April 28 — UEC +9.68%, X-Energy +23.22% — as nuclear power gains credibility as baseload AI energy solution. Citi and BofA added uranium to strategy notes.

metrics.label: US 10Y Yield
metrics.change: Backed down from 4.6%+ war peak as oil eased

metrics.label: USD/SGD
metrics.change: SGD weakened 1.12% MoM; elevated on USD strength

metrics.label: Fed Funds Rate
metrics.change: On hold April 29; statement acknowledges ME uncertainty

usGlobal.what: NVDA fiscal Q4 $68.1B revenue (+73% YoY); Q1 FY26 guidance $78B. Reports May 20.
usGlobal.why: AI infrastructure capex from hyperscalers remains in acceleration phase. Fiscal Q4 results confirm Blackwell/GH200 demand sustained.
usGlobal.implication: AI Infra thesis validated. Any guidance cut would be catastrophic for portfolio's 16% AI Infra allocation. Monitor closely.

usGlobal.what: Trump raised EU auto tariffs to 25% on May 1, accusing EU of non-compliance.
usGlobal.why: Escalation of transatlantic trade tensions. European luxury/auto sector most exposed. USD strengthens on safe-haven flows — SGD cross-pressure.
usGlobal.implication: Stronger USD = SGD weakens = USD-denominated assets inflate SGD value but import costs rise. Singapore banks with USD exposure benefit but EM sentiment could soften regional markets.

usGlobal.what: April S&P 500 +10%, Nasdaq +15% — best monthly gains in years. Earnings-driven.
usGlobal.why: Strong corporate earnings offset oil supply shock. Tech mega-caps (Apple, Microsoft, Amazon) led the rally.
usGlobal.implication: Risk-on environment supports portfolio's 59.6% risk assets. Momentum favors continuation but May is historically weak seasonally.

singapore.what: USD/SGD at 1.2719 (SGD weakened 1.12% MoM, up 2.15% YoY). SGD near 12-month low vs USD.
singapore.why: USD strengthened on Fed pause + safe-haven flows from Middle East uncertainty. SGD tracking regional currencies lower.
singapore.implication: For SGD 2.618M portfolio, USD strength inflates SGD value of USD-denominated holdings. 26% cash (682k SGD) effectively buys fewer USD assets if USD/SGD rises further — consider accumulating USD ETFs on SGD weakness.

singapore.what: Singapore REITs: Ascendas, CICT, MLT, FCT — flat to slightly negative last 30 days.
singapore.why: Rate environment keeps REIT yields uncompetitive. S-Reits average yield ~6-7% vs 4-4.5% 10Y UST.
singapore.implication: Reits under pressure as long as Fed holds. Distribution yields attractive but total return limited in near term. Accumulate on dips for yield.

commodities.what: Oil: WTI collapsed from $111+ to ~$100.69 (-10.4% in days). Ceasefire + Iran said Strait of Hormuz open.
commodities.why: Geopolitical risk premium partially unwound. 1st order: oil down. But 2nd order: inflation fears ease → Fed stays on hold → real rates don't spike → growth assets supported.
commodities.implication: Oil decline is NET POSITIVE for portfolio. Lower energy costs = lower inflation = Fed credibility maintained = equity multiples expand. Don't let the 'oil = inflation' headline fool you.

commodities.what: Gold at ~$4,644-4,659. Elevated but off war highs of $4,700+.
commodities.why: Gold is a REAL RATE hedge, NOT a crisis hedge. Real yields back to ~1.8-2% after oil decline. Gold range-bound $4,500-4,700.
commodities.implication: Gold position (7% = 175k SGD) appropriate. No addition at current prices — real rates not deeply negative enough to drive re-rating. Hold.

commodities.what: Uranium: UEC +9.68%, X-Energy +23.22% on April 28. Citi added uranium to illiquid allocation.
commodities.why: AI power demand + energy security → nuclear power comeback. GPT-4 class data centers need 10-20MW; nuclear is only viable zero-carbon baseload at scale.
commodities.implication: GRID rally validates uranium thesis. Nuclear power is missing link in AI energy narrative — watch for more institutional additions.

narratives.title: AI Infrastructure Capex: The Unstoppable Theme
narratives.description: TSMC Q1 profit +58% YoY; $52-56B capex 2026. NVDA guiding $78B next quarter. Hyperscalers collectively spending $300B+ on AI infrastructure in 2026. The 'own what AI depends on' thesis — semis, equipment, energy — confirmed by earnings.

narratives.title: Strait of Hormuz: Ceasefire But Effectively Closed
narratives.description: Iran declared strait open April 17 under ceasefire, oil fell 10%. But almost no commercial shipping has used it — US counter-blockade ongoing. Ceasefire extended but fragile. Oil supply shock risk de-risked but not eliminated.

narratives.title: Fed Pause With Inflation Still Above Target
narratives.description: Fed held at 3.5-3.75% on April 29. CPI at +3.1% YoY (March). 10-year expected inflation: 2.4%. Fed in 'higher for longer' stance but oil decline gives breathing room. Markets pricing 1-2 cuts in 2026.
narratives.source: FOMC statement, BLS CPI, Cleveland Fed

narratives.title: Transatlantic Trade Tension: EU Auto Tariffs 25%
narratives.description: Trump tore up partial EU tariff deal May 1, raising auto tariffs to 25%. Accused EU of non-compliance. Slow-burn risk — EU large trading bloc, retaliatory tariffs possible. 2nd order: USD strengthens → emerging markets weaken → SGD tracks lower.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil collapse from $111 to ~$100: 1st order = energy inflation fears ease. 2nd order = Fed credibility maintained, no 'last mile' inflation shock. 3rd order = Real rates stable ~1.8-2% → gold range-bound $4,500-4,700, equities get multiple expansion. PORTFOLIO EFFECT: Net positive. Lower energy costs = corporate/consumer tax cut.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Don't panic-sell energy holdings on oil dip. 2nd/3rd order is constructive for risk assets. Energy sector (7% of portfolio) underperforms oil price directly but macro offset is positive.

secondOrderEffects.text: EU tariffs to 25%: 1st order = European auto/luxury stocks crushed. 2nd order = EUR weakens → DXY strengthens → USD/SGD toward 1.28+. 3rd order = Singapore exports to US more competitive but SGD weakening inflationary for imports. SGD portfolio with 40% USD assets = modest USD tailwind in SGD terms.
secondOrderEffects.implication: EWY (Korea) at risk if trade war spreads. Singapore bank USD assets benefit but regional bank contagion risk rises.

secondOrderEffects.text: TSMC $52-56B capex 2026: 1st order = ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, MU all get incremental orders. 2nd order = AI chip production capacity expands. 3rd order = CoWoS advanced packaging as valuable as chips themselves. TSMC controls 50%+ of high-end CoWoS market.
secondOrderEffects.implication: ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC are picks-and-shovels of AI Infra. ASML crossing $500B market cap signals equipment cycle not over.

secondOrderEffects.text: Uranium rally: 1st order = UEC, X-Energy surge. 2nd order = data center power demands make nuclear only viable zero-carbon baseload. 3rd order = uranium price rises → mining capex eventually responds → 5-7 year supply deficit structural. Decade-long theme.
secondOrderEffects.implication: GRID position appropriate. Pure-play uranium (UEC, CCJ) illiquid and volatile. Thesis correct but patience required. Nuclear regulatory timelines are long.

investment.thesis: Accumulate AI infrastructure equipment names (ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) on any May pullback. TSMC capex $52-56B confirms demand pipeline through 2026. May historically weak seasonal — use it to build positions.
investment.trigger: S&P 500 pullback of 3-5% in May typical seasonal pattern. Equipment names lag chip makers — room to run.
investment.whyNow: AI Infra 16% of portfolio (420k SGD) is underweight vs thematic importance. PE ratios of equipment names still reasonable vs chip designers.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex guidance cut for 2026. TSMC cutting 2026 capex below $45B. AI server demand saturation.

investment.thesis: Hold gold position (175k SGD = 7%) as real rate hedge. Don't add at $4,650+. Real rates ~1.8-2% don't justify higher gold. Crisis = gold narrative is wrong — gold needs deeply negative real rates to re-rate.
investment.trigger: Gold below $4,400 = buying opportunity (real rates falling via inflation rising OR Fed cutting faster).
investment.whyNow: Current allocation appropriate. Oil easing means inflation expectations won't spike, supporting gold at $4,500-4,700.
investment.invalidator: Fed pivots to aggressive cuts (50bp+) → real rates deeply negative → gold to $5,000+.

trading.thesis: Watch USD/SGD closely. If crosses 1.28, SGD-denominated investors accumulate VWRA/QQQ/NVDA more aggressively. USD strength temporary (trade war risk premium) — FX forwards suggest eventual normalization.
trading.trigger: USD/SGD > 1.28 triggers accelerated USD asset accumulation using cash (682k SGD, 26%).
trading.whyNow: SGD at weakest vs USD in 12 months. 26% cash earning ~3.8% in SGD deposits underperforming USD assets. Consider converting to USD ETFs for 2+ year horizon.
trading.invalidator: SGD strengthens sharply on risk-off reversal; Fed cuts aggressively.

trading.thesis: EU auto tariff escalation early-stage. European equities (EWY, FXE) could fall 5-10% more. Avoid adding exposure until clarity on retaliatory tariffs.
trading.trigger: EWY below 72 or FXE below 98 signals trade war escalation getting serious.
trading.whyNow: Portfolio has EWY but not direct EU equity. European slowdown → weaker EUR → stronger USD → indirect SGD impact.
trading.invalidator: EU and US negotiate new deal; tariff escalation reversed.

watchlist.thesis: NVDA earnings May 20 — fiscal Q1 FY26 guidance $78B. Any guidance below $72B significant negative for AI Infra and broader Nasdaq. Portfolio's 16% AI Infra directly exposed.
watchlist.trigger: Watch Blackwell GPU demand signals, hyperscaler capex commentary, China export license developments.
watchlist.whyNow: NVDA most important single-stock event for this portfolio. Guidance cut cascades through entire AI stack.
watchlist.invalidator: Guidance above $80B confirms AI infrastructure supercycle intact.

watchlist.thesis: Strait of Hormuz ceasefire expiration risk. Ceasefire extended but effectively closed. If hostilities resume, oil spikes to $110+ → 1st order: stagflation fear. 2nd order: Fed can't cut → real rates rise → gold crushed vs cash. Asymmetric tail risk.
watchlist.trigger: Any attack on commercial shipping in Strait. Oil above $108 = ceasefire failure signal.
watchlist.whyNow: VIX at 17 (elevated). Asymmetric risk: resumed hostilities extremely negative for 60% risk-asset portfolio.
watchlist.invalidator: Formal peace treaty signed; Strait reopens for commercial shipping.

riskWatch.name: Strait of Hormuz Ceasefire Failure
riskWatch.description: Ceasefire extended but effectively closed. If hostilities resume, oil returns to $110+ and macro regime shifts to 'stagflation risk' — bad for equities, potentially bad for gold (real rate spike).
riskWatch.mitigation: 26% cash (682k SGD) provides dry powder to buy dips. Energy sector (7%) cushions oil-linked assets but hurts growth equity. Monitor TLT as hedge if ceasefire alarm bells ring.

riskWatch.name: USD/SGD Spike to 1.28+ (EU Trade War → USD Strength)
riskWatch.description: EU tariffs 25% on autos = 1st order EUR weakness, 2nd order USD strength. SGD tracks lower. Portfolio has 40%+ in USD-denominated assets — in SGD terms windfall but export competitiveness concerns for Singapore.
riskWatch.mitigation: Some SGD cash selectively deployed into USD ETFs (VWRA, QQQ). Keep 3-6 months SGD expenses in SGD; rest in USD for long-term investors.

riskWatch.name: NVDA Guidance Cut May 20
riskWatch.description: NVDA guiding $78B for fiscal Q1 2026. Guidance below $72B = significant mark-to-market for 16% AI Infra. Entire AI stack (NVDA→AVGO→TSM→ASML→AMAT→LRCX→KLAC→MU→SNPS) would reprice.
riskWatch.mitigation: Don't concentrate further into NVDA at current prices. Diversified AI Infra allocation (multiple names across stack) provides protection. QQQ/VWRA provide broad index exposure.

keyEvents.text: Fed holds rates at 3.5-3.75% — April 29 FOMC; Powell to remain; statement flags Middle East uncertainty
Trump raises EU auto tariffs to 25%, tearing up partial trade deal — May 1
S&P 500 +10.1%, Nasdaq +15.2% in April — best monthly gains in years
Oil collapsed ~10% from $111+ peak to ~$100.69 on ceasefire news; Strait of Hormuz declared open by Iran
TSMC Q1 profit +58% YoY; 2026 capex guidance $52-56B
NVDA fiscal Q4 $68.1B revenue (+73% YoY); guiding Q1 FY26 $78B; reports May 20
Uranium stocks surge: UEC +9.68%, X-Energy +23.22% on April 28; Citi adds uranium to allocation

whatMatters: NVDA earnings May 20 — single most important event for portfolio's AI Infra thesis
Strait of Hormuz ceasefire status — oil above $108 signals resumption of hostilities risk
USD/SGD cross — if above 1.28, convert more SGD cash to USD assets
EU trade escalation — watch for retaliatory tariff announcements that could spill into Asian markets
whatToDo: Hold AI Infra positions — TSMC capex confirms buildout is real and sustained
Accumulate any May seasonal weakness in QQQ/VWRA/equipment names — seasonal pattern historically creates entry points
Monitor USD/SGD > 1.27 as signal to increase USD asset accumulation using SGD cash
Hold gold at 7% — no addition needed at current levels; real rates not deeply negative enough
Uranium thesis confirmed — GRID hold, watch for pullback to add
whatToIgnore: Daily oil price swings of <3% — 2nd order effect (inflation ease → Fed credibility → equity support) more important than 1st order energy sector impact
'Sell in May' narrative — AI infrastructure capex cycle is not seasonal; earnings drive markets, not calendars
Gold crisis narrative — gold is a real rate asset, not a crisis asset. Current oil/economy mix is constructive for gold at $4,600
EU auto tariff headline panic — direct portfolio exposure minimal; indirect FX effect is real risk, which is manageable
